commonIssues:
Typographical errors in the application form (e.g., name spelling, date of birth) often result in errors on the final certificate.

How to fix:

  • Verify all details against original documents (Aadhaar, PAN) before submission.
  • If an error has already been made, submit an affidavit for correction along with supporting documents at your respective ward office or online via the IGR Maharashtra Portal.

The registration process, especially under the Special Marriage Act, can take 30–45 days and requires a 30-day waiting period where a public notice is displayed.

How to fix:

  • Track your application online through the Aaple Sarkar Portal.
  • Ensure there are no legal objections filed during the 30-day notice period to avoid further delays.

Finding three witnesses who can appear in person and provide local proof of address can be challenging.

How to fix:

  • Ensure all three witnesses have valid local government-issued ID (like Aadhaar or Voter ID).
  • If a witness cannot attend, you must submit a fresh application with new witnesses or provide legal representation where permitted.

Women who adopt a new first and last name after marriage often face intense difficulties updating their name on Aadhaar and PAN cards, which can freeze bank accounts or delay travel documents.

How to fix:

  • First, apply for a name change in the official Maharashtra State Gazette.
  • Use the Gazette notification and the marriage certificate to sequentially update Aadhaar, then PAN, then Passport.

Submitting the application to the wrong ward office based on the marriage location is a frequent mistake.

How to fix:

  • Check your exact ward jurisdiction using your residential pin code on the BMC Portal (for Mumbai) or local municipal website before applying.

Confusion regarding the specific law (Hindu Marriage Act vs. Special Marriage Act) and the need for a religious priest/officiant for verification.

How to fix:

  • Use the Hindu Marriage Act only if both parties are Hindu, Buddhist, Jain, or Sikh, and provide the priest's certificate. For inter-religious marriages, you must apply under the Special Marriage Act.
